seoTitle: How to Remove a Course From Your Coursera Dashboard
metaDescription: Clean up your Coursera dashboard by removing a course you no longer want visible, in just a couple of minutes with these simple steps.
intro: A dashboard full of courses you enrolled in by accident or no longer care about makes it harder to focus on what you're actually studying. This covers finding the course, removing it from your dashboard, and confirming the change so your view stays organized.

steps.heading: Open your Coursera dashboard
steps.body: Sign in and go to your dashboard where all enrolled courses are displayed.

steps.heading: Find the course you want to remove
steps.body: Locate the specific course cluttering your view and open its options.

steps.heading: Remove the course from your view
steps.body: Select the option to remove or hide the course from your active dashboard.

steps.heading: Review your updated dashboard
steps.body: Check that your dashboard now only shows the courses you actually want to see.

faq.question: Does removing a course from my dashboard delete my progress?
faq.answer: Removing it from view is mainly about decluttering your dashboard; check Coursera's specifics if you're concerned about losing recorded progress.

faq.question: Can I add the course back later if I change my mind?
faq.answer: In most cases you can re-enroll or add the course back if you decide you want it visible again.

faq.question: Is this useful for corporate trainers managing multiple learners?
faq.answer: Yes, keeping course visibility organized is especially useful for corporate trainers managing several courses across a team.

faq.question: How long does removing a course take?
faq.answer: It's a quick process, generally taking just a couple of minutes once you find the right settings.
